user_query:
I’m tracking which reader themes are rising this week so I can pitch a timely piece and write to my subscribers. Please work entirely within the local workspace and produce three artifacts.

Inputs to scan:
- Inspect the directories input/inbox/ and input/notes/ and process the following if present:
  1) Any CSV files matching input/inbox/comments_*.csv (columns: id,timestamp,source,text). This week includes input/inbox/comments_week.csv.
  2) The JSONL file input/inbox/newsletter_replies.jsonl (one JSON object per line with fields: email,timestamp,body).
  3) The Markdown file input/notes/reader_polls.md (contains simple "Label: Count" lines).

Watchlist phrases (case-insensitive phrase matches):
- quiet luxury
- dark academia
- liminal spaces (also count "liminal space")
- AI co-authors (also count any of: "ai co-author", "ai co-authors", "ai coauthor", "ai coauthors", "ai co-authorship")
- typewriter(s) (count "typewriter" and "typewriters")
- pseudonym(s) (count "pseudonym" and "pseudonyms")

Poll label mapping (map these labels in reader_polls.md to the above phrases for poll counts):
- Quiet luxury -> quiet luxury
- Dark academia -> dark academia
- Liminal spaces -> liminal spaces
- AI co-authorship -> AI co-authors
- Typewriters -> typewriter(s)
- Hidden identity/pseudonym -> pseudonym(s)

What to produce:
1) A trends report at output/trends/weekly_trends.json with this structure:
   - sources: a list of objects for each processed input file with fields {path, type in {comments_csv|replies_jsonl|polls_md}, records_count}.
   - phrase_stats: for each watchlist phrase, an object with fields:
     - phrase
     - total_occurrences (sum across comments + replies + poll counts)
     - by_source: {comments: integer, replies: integer, polls: integer}
     - example_snippets: include up to 2 short snippets from comments and up to 2 from replies that contain the matched phrase (omit for polls). Each snippet should include fields {source_type in {comment|reply}, source_id or email, text_excerpt}.
   - top_phrases_by_total: the watchlist phrases sorted by total_occurrences (descending).

   Notes:
   - Count only contiguous phrase matches as defined above (case-insensitive). Apply the provided alias rules for AI co-author variants and plural/singular as listed.
   - For polls, use the mapped label counts directly; do not derive snippets.

2) An editor pitch email draft at output/drafts/editor_pitch.txt that includes:
   - A Subject line that mentions the top 3 phrases by total_occurrences.
   - 1–2 short paragraphs proposing article angles grounded in the findings, with a nod to my belief in an ancestral/"mysterious author" voice guiding my creativity.
   - A bullet list summarizing the top 3 phrases with their total counts and one piece of evidence each (cite either a comment or a reply snippet and identify the source type).

3) A subscriber note at output/drafts/subscriber_note.txt that includes:
   - A warm opening referencing the same ancestral muse idea.
   - A brief summary of the top 2 phrases and why they matter.
   - A clear call to action inviting replies on which theme readers want next week.

Make sure to:
- Inspect the directories to discover and enumerate the actual input files you process (reflect this in the report’s sources array).
- Read and extract textual content from each file type as specified.
- Save all three outputs to the exact paths above; create any missing directories under output/.

I’ll review the JSON report and both drafts together to decide the next piece. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
